title: quercus coccifera
intro: Quercus coccifera, also known as the dwarf oak or kermes oak, is a species of evergreen tree or shrub native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be attributed to the presence of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and tannins. The health benefits of Quercus coccifera include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and exhibiting antimicrobial and antioxidant properties. Herbal preparations of Quercus coccifera include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various ailments, such as wounds, skin conditions, and gastrointestinal issues.
